title: Semantic lint aggregate proof lane
description: Aggregate proof-lane contract for the asupersync-specific semantic lint suite. The lane ties the rule inventory, seven per-rule fixture contracts, ASUP diagnostic code registry checks, summary/events metadata, manifest mapping, and no-claim boundaries to one remote-required RCH command.
proof_command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_semantic_lint_proof_lane CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -j 1 -p asupersync --test semantic_lint_proof_lane_contract --test semantic_lint_rule_inventory_contract --test semantic_lint_diagnostic_codes_contract --test error_code_registry_contract --test semantic_lint_ambient_contract --test semantic_lint_cleanup_budget_contract --test semantic_lint_core_tokio_contract --test semantic_lint_loop_checkpoint_contract --test semantic_lint_ignored_outcome_contract --test semantic_lint_await_holding_contract --test semantic_lint_drop_race_loser_contract -- --nocapture

default_surface_policy.intended_default_surface: The aggregate lane covers the rule target paths and fixture classes declared by each per-rule contract. Full repository scans remain rule-specific and must be cited from the individual lint results, not inferred from this aggregate metadata verifier.
zero_false_positive_basis: negative fixtures pass in each per-rule contract
valid allow-marker fixtures suppress only with reason and owner metadata
invalid allow-marker fixtures fail and do not suppress findings
core Tokio graph carve-outs are checked by explicit negative fixtures
unclassified_suppression_ratcheting: allow markers must start with asupersync-lint:allow
allow markers must name the exact rule id
allow markers must include reason= and owner=
owner metadata must match an asupersync bead-style owner

failure_rehearsal: positive fixtures produce fail verdicts with findings
invalid allow-marker fixtures produce invalid_allow_marker findings
core-tokio positive default graph fixture reports ASUP-E908
no_claims: A green semantic-lint proof lane is not a formal proof of cancel-correctness.
A green semantic-lint proof lane does not certify broad workspace health.
A green semantic-lint proof lane does not certify release readiness.
A green semantic-lint proof lane does not prove performance, live RCH fleet availability, or complete absence of semantic issues outside the declared rule contract surfaces.
Checked summary/events artifacts with rerun-required status are not fresh RCH evidence until the exact manifest command is rerun remotely.
